generated_oml:
// C2: Acting Components
instance GantryCraneController : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Controller for real-time operation and coordination of the lab-scale gantry crane, enabling bidirectional data exchange with the digital twin for closed-loop control and validation."
]

instance HoistMotorController : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Controller for the hoist mechanism motors driving the crane's vertical movement, interfacing with the electromagnet for container puck manipulation."
]

instance SwingMotorController : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Controller for the hoist's swinging motion motors, managing the horizontal positioning of the crane arm."
]

// C3: Physical sensing components
instance SwingAngleEncoder : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Encoder measuring the hoist's swinging motion position (kinematic data of the crane's arm)."
    DTDFVocab:producedData SwingAngleData
]

instance Anemometer : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Sensor measuring environmental wind speed (applicable in operational contexts)."
    DTDFVocab:producedData EnvironmentalFactorData
]

// C4: Physical-to-virtual interaction
instance SwingAngleData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom SwingAngleEncoder
]

instance EnvironmentalFactorData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom Anemometer
]

instance CraneStateData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom SwingAngleEncoder,
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom HoistMotorController,
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom SwingMotorController
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance GantryCraneCADModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Geometric CAD model of the gantry crane structure (gantry beam, hoist mechanism, container puck carriers) derived from Autodesk Fusion360 for visualization and kinematic analysis."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo TimescaleDBEnabler
]

instance KinematicModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Python-based kinematic model simulating crane movement and trajectory planning for real-time synchronization with physical system."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo PythonServiceEnabler
]

instance TrajectorySimulationData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Precomputed kinematic simulations for trajectory prediction, validated against physical measurements via continuous validation service."
    DTDFVocab:fromData CraneStateData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo GrafanaDashboardEnabler
]

instance OperationalStateData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Real-time state metrics (motion status, load-bearing readiness) transmitted from physical crane controllers to digital twin."
    DTDFVocab:fromData CraneStateData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo TimescaleDBEnabler
]

instance PositionData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Real-time positional data (swing angle, hoist position) derived from sensor readings for closed-loop control."
    DTDFVocab:fromData SwingAngleData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo PythonServiceEnabler
]

// C11: Enablers
instance PythonServiceEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Primary Python-based enabler for microservices (gantrylib module) handling trajectory generation, control logic, and real-time data processing."
    DTDFVocab:enables TrajectoryGeneratorService,
    DTDFVocab:enables SimulationService,
    DTDFVocab:enables ContinuousValidationService
]

instance TimescaleDBEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Time-series database (TimescaleDB) for continuous storage and querying of operational data (sensor readings, control commands) with implicit preprocessing via Python interface."
    DTDFVocab:enables HistoricalDataLoggingService,
    DTDFVocab:enables ContinuousValidationService
]

instance GrafanaDashboardEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Visualization platform (Grafana) for real-time dashboard visualization of operational metrics, sensor data, and performance trends."
    DTDFVocab:enables DataVisualizationService
]

instance DockerContainerEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Containerization platform (Docker) enabling modular deployment and scalability of Python microservices for dynamic extension."
    DTDFVocab:enables OptimalControlService,
    DTDFVocab:enables TrajectoryGeneratorService
]

// C6: Services
instance OptimalControlService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Real-time kinematic and dynamic optimization service for enhancing crane movement efficiency and safety through controller feedback integration."
    DTDFVocab:provides AdaptiveTrajectoryOptimizationInsight
]

instance HistoricalDataLoggingService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Continuous recording and structured storage of physical system states (position, velocity, load) in TimescaleDB for offline diagnostics and trend analysis."
    DTDFVocab:provides LoadDistributionTrendAnalysisInsight
]

instance DataVisualizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Interactive 3D visualization service combining CAD model overlays with real-time sensor data and simulation predictions for error detection."
    DTDFVocab:provides RealTimeTrajectoryComparisonAction
]

instance ContinuousValidationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Real-time synchronization service comparing physical and digital states to detect kinematic mismatches and sensor errors via DarTwin notation."
    DTDFVocab:provides KinematicConsistencyCheckInsight
]

instance TrajectoryGeneratorService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service computing suboptimal crane trajectories via simulation to ensure safety and efficiency, integrated with controller feedback loops."
    DTDFVocab:provides OptimalTrajectoryInsight
]

instance SimulationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Simulation service implementing kinematic models for trajectory prediction and validation against physical measurements."
    DTDFVocab:provides SimulationValidationInsight
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance AdaptiveTrajectoryOptimizationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Real-time optimization insights quantifying efficiency improvements and safety margins through dynamic trajectory adjustments."
]

instance LoadDistributionTrendAnalysisInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Historical trend analysis of load distribution on crane arms derived from TimescaleDB time-series data for operational efficiency assessment."
]

instance RealTimeTrajectoryComparisonAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Visualization action enabling dynamic comparison between predicted and actual crane trajectories for model validation."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance KinematicConsistencyCheckInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Real-time consistency check quantifying prediction reliability through simulation-derived bounds (e.g., confidence intervals) for error detection."
]

instance OptimalTrajectoryInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Simulation-derived optimal trajectory recommendations with confidence bounds for safe and efficient crane operations."
]

instance SimulationValidationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Validation metrics comparing physical measurements with simulation predictions to assess model fidelity and synchronization accuracy."
]

// C1: Environment
instance LaboratoryEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:contains GantryCraneController,
    base:contains HoistMotorController,
    base:contains SwingMotorController,
    base:contains SwingAngleEncoder,
    base:contains Anemometer,
    base:contains PythonServiceEnabler,
    base:contains TimescaleDBEnabler,
    base:contains GrafanaDashboardEnabler,
    base:contains DockerContainerEnabler
]

instance PhysicalCraneSystem : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Lab-scale gantry crane system with open-source controller integration, designed for research validation and educational demonstrations in cyber-physical systems.",
    base:contains GantryCraneController,
    base:contains HoistMotorController,
    base:contains SwingMotorController,
    base:contains SwingAngleEncoder,
    base:contains Anemometer
]

instance DigitalTwinSystem : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Digital twin implementation of the lab-scale gantry crane comprising geometric CAD models, kinematic simulations, and real-time synchronization services.",
    base:contains GantryCraneCADModel,
    base:contains KinematicModel,
    base:contains TrajectorySimulationData,
    base:contains OperationalStateData,
    base:contains PositionData,
    base:contains OptimalControlService,
    base:contains HistoricalDataLoggingService,
    base:contains DataVisualizationService,
    base:contains ContinuousValidationService,
    base:contains TrajectoryGeneratorService,
    base:contains SimulationService
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "
The **virtual_to_physical_interaction** involves the **Control Application** and **Trajectory Generator Service** sending commands to the **Physical Crane** via the **Physical Crane Interface**. Key aspects include:

**Command Types**:
- **Trajectory Execution Commands**: Generated by the **Trajectory Generator Service** (using kinematics) to define optimal paths with zero residual swinging motion. These commands are relayed to the crane’s controllers.
- **Real-Time Adjustments**: The **Continuous Validation Service** may trigger feedback loops to correct deviations (e.g., via the **Dashboard**’s operator inputs).

**Control Message Formats**:
- **Implicitly Python-based** (e.g., JSON/Protocol Buffers) for inter-service communication, though the exact format is not detailed. The **MQTT Broker** likely encodes commands as lightweight messages for efficiency.

**Validation and Authentication**:
- **Validation**: The **Trajectory Generator Service** validates trajectories against kinematic constraints before sending them to the crane. The **Continuous Validation Service** compares simulated vs. physical outcomes post-execution.
- **Authentication**: Not explicitly mentioned; assumes trusted operator inputs via the **Human Machine Interface (HMI)**.

**Feedback Systems**:
- The **Continuous Validation Service** provides **distance metrics** (e.g., Euclidean error) between simulated and actual trajectories. If thresholds are breached, the **Dashboard** notifies operators, who can override commands via the HMI.

**Conflict Resolution**:
- No explicit conflict resolution mechanism is described. Potential resolution occurs via operator intervention (e.g., aborting trajectories) or automated threshold-based adjustments.

**Command Execution Confirmation**:
- Implied by the **Continuous Validation Service**’s post-execution comparison, where discrepancies trigger alerts. No explicit acknowledgment protocol is detailed.
"
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The GreenhouseDT exemplar relies on **InfluxDB** for time-series data storage, optimized for real-time status monitoring and adaptive behavior synchronization between the physical greenhouse and its digital twin. The system does not specify explicit synchronization frequencies or latency requirements, but the use of **ActiveMQ** as a message broker implies asynchronous event-driven communication with minimal latency for critical adaptations. The Air Quality Management exemplar leverages **Microsoft Azure tools**, which inherently support low-latency real-time processing for runtime visualization, simulation, and prediction applications, though no specific time scales or synchronization granularity are detailed. The lab-scale gantry crane system (Document 2, III) is not described in terms of temporal aspects, as its architecture diagram (C4 Model) does not include synchronization or latency specifications."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The **GreenhouseDT** system employs a **low-cost, self-adaptive digital twin** orchestrated via **InfluxDB** (time-series database) and **ActiveMQ** (message broker), enabling behavioral adaptation between physical and digital components without explicit constellation patterns. The **Air Quality Management** exemplar uses **AutomationML** for digital twin modeling and **Microsoft Azure tools** (e.g., runtime visualization, simulation, prediction) to coordinate services, though no specific architecture patterns (e.g., microservices, event-driven) are detailed. The **lab-scale gantry crane** (Document 2, III) is depicted using the **C4 Model**, revealing a **component-based constellation** with clear integration points: an **MQTT Broker** for signaling, a **Database** for persistent data, and a **Simulation Service** for predictive modeling. Coordination relies on **C4’s container diagram**, illustrating a centralized orchestration of physical and digital components."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "
  **Lifecycle Phases for Lab Scale Gantry Crane Digital Twin:**
  - **Development Phase**:
    - Representation: The digital twin is implemented using a **Python-based `gantrylib` module** for core functionality, containerized via **Docker** for modular deployment.
    - Governance: Hosted on GitHub with pre-recorded data in virtual machine images, enabling reproducible research (used in academic publications).
    - Transition Management: The **C4 Model** (Context and Container diagrams) formalizes architecture transitions between physical and digital components, ensuring alignment with cyber-physical system requirements.

  **Representation Types**:
    - **Physical Crane**: Real-time sensor data (e.g., position, trajectory) mapped via IoT protocols (e.g., Eclipse Mosquitto).
    - **Simulation Service**: Trajectory validation via **TimescaleDB** for time-series data, compared against executed trajectories for fault detection.
    - **Dashboard**: Grafana-based visualization with confidence bounds derived from simulations (Fig. 8).

  **Lifecycle Governance**:
    - Continuous validation via **Continuous Validation Service** (Section III.A), ensuring real-time synchronization between physical and digital states.
    - Research-driven evolution: Leverages **DarTwin notation** for system evolution (Section I, IV.A) to adapt to new operational constraints.

  **Not in Document**: No explicit lifecycle standards or certification frameworks are mentioned for this system.
"
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "
The Digital Twin employs a **Continuous Validation Service** that:
- Compares real-time measurements of executed trajectories with simulated trajectories.
- Calculates distance metrics between the two sets of data and triggers alerts if deviations exceed an acceptable threshold.

No explicit mention of uncertainty quantification, Monte Carlo simulations, or pre-deployment validation methods is provided. The documents only describe the real-time validation process without elaborating on broader fidelity assurance processes.
"
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "
The **digital twin’s technical network infrastructure** operates as a **single-node, centralized setup** on a **personal computer**, with no explicit mention of distributed architectures or multi-node redundancy. The **communication protocols** include:
1. **Database connection**: **PostgreSQL protocol** (implied by TimescaleDB’s underlying PostgreSQL backend) for storing and retrieving crane state/logs.
2. **Inter-service signaling**: **MQTT broker** (e.g., Mosquitto or similar) for asynchronous messaging between services (e.g., Trajectory Generator Service ↔ Simulation Service ↔ Continuous Validation Service).

**Network topology** is **centralized**, with all components (Database, Dashboard, Services) co-located on the same host. **Bandwidth/latency requirements** are not specified, but the **Continuous Validation Service**’s real-time distance metric calculations imply **low-latency (<100 ms)** requirements for trajectory feedback loops. **Reliability mechanisms** are minimal; redundancy is not mentioned, and the system operates on a **single-node basis**.

**Security protocols** are not detailed, but the absence of explicit encryption or authentication suggests a **basic, unencrypted** communication model for this lab-scale deployment. Proprietary hardware protocols for the Physical Crane Interface are **not specified** in the documents.
"
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "
The **digital twin deployment infrastructure** is **on-premise and single-node**, running entirely on a **personal computer** (as implied by the context diagram and containerized services). The system is **not cloud-based or edge-deployed** and lacks explicit scalability or high-availability requirements.

**Hosting Platforms**:
- **Cloud/Edge/On-Premise**: The entire stack (Database, MQTT Broker, Dashboard, Trajectory Generator, Simulation Service, Continuous Validation Service) is deployed **on-premise** on a single PC.
- **Computational Requirements**:
  - The **Trajectory Generator Service** and **Simulation Service** rely on **Python-based kinematics models**, but no specific CPU requirements (e.g., high-performance CPUs) are mentioned.
  - The **Logging Application** and **Dashboard** (Grafana) are lightweight services with minimal computational demands.
- **Storage Requirements**:
  - The **TimescaleDB** database stores historical and real-time crane data but no explicit storage capacity (e.g., 100+ GB) is specified.
- **Scalability Mechanisms**:
  - The system is **not designed for horizontal scaling** (e.g., Docker swarm, Kubernetes) as it operates in a single-node environment.
  - The **containerized deployment** (Docker) allows modular updates but does not imply scalability beyond the single PC.
- **Deployment Models**:
  - The **containerized deployment** (Docker) is used for the **Database, MQTT Broker, and Dashboard**, but the **Control Application, Logging Application, and Physical Crane Interface** are not explicitly containerized (implied by their Python-based nature).
- **Availability/Reliability Assurance**:
  - The document does not mention redundancy, backup mechanisms, or uptime guarantees. The system relies on the **single-node PC** and **TimescaleDB** (which may support replication if configured, but not described).
"
]

instance horizontal_integration : DTDFVocab:HoriIntegration [
    base:desc "The **GreenhouseDT** exemplar does not mention integration with external systems, though it relies on **InfluxDB** and **ActiveMQ** for internal data exchange. The **Air Quality Management** exemplar integrates with **Microsoft Azure tools** (e.g., runtime visualization, prediction) via **AutomationML** for interoperability with construction engineering systems and **Raspberry-Pi sensors** for real-time data collection. The **lab-scale gantry crane** (Document 2, III) integrates via **MQTT Broker** for signaling between physical and digital components, with no explicit mention of enterprise integration or custom APIs. No evidence of **OPC UA**, **Logic Apps**, or **Stream Analytics** is found; only **AutomationML** and **Azure tools** are referenced."
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "
  **Standardization for Lab Scale Gantry Crane:**
  - **Technologies**:
    - **Architecture**: **C4 Model** (Context/Container diagrams) for system decomposition (Section III.A).
    - **Data Storage**: **TimescaleDB** (standard for time-series databases) and **Grafana** (open-source visualization).
    - **IoT Protocols**: **Eclipse Mosquitto** (MQTT broker) for sensor data exchange.
  - **Ad-Hoc Framework**:
    - No commercial digital twin frameworks (e.g., Siemens MindSphere, PTC ThingWorx) are used; instead, a **custom pipeline** leverages Docker containers and Python modules.
    - **Domain-Specific Standards**: No explicit compliance with **IEC 61499** or **ISO/IEC 23270** (Digital Twin standards) mentioned; focus is on research reproducibility.
  **Not in Document**: No certification requirements or interoperability frameworks (e.g., OPC UA) are documented.
"
]
